pr.title: particle.c: decompile 35 stubs, push 5 to 100%, improve 52 functions
pr.author: malvarezcastillo

summary: Large particle.c decompilation PR that replaced 35 stubs, improved many existing functions, and raised the particle translation unit's text match from 22.57% to 91.45% according to the author. The work also updated particle headers/static declarations, renamed several BSS particle globals, marked many local data/BSS symbols as local in symbols.txt, and adjusted downstream users in dballoc.c, eflib.c, itseakchain.c, hsd_3A94.h, objalloc.h, and psstructs.h. The key review/author discussion documented that remaining non-100% objdiff gaps were instruction-identical and caused by relocation metadata: same-TU BSS references emitted as .bss.0 versus named target symbols, and source-order .data string/jumptable layout shifts.
what_changed: Decompiled 35 stubs in src/sysdolphin/baselib/particle.c and improved many other functions in the same translation unit.
According to the author, final stats versus master were 7 new 100% matches, 51 improved functions, .text section match improvement from 22.57% to 91.45%, and +1788 bytes matched code.
Added substantial implementation code to particle.c, with the PR slice reporting 6798 added and 317 deleted lines in that file.
Moved or consolidated particle-related declarations across particle.h, particle.static.h, psstructs.h, hsd_3A94.h, and objalloc.h.
Updated config/GALE01/symbols.txt so many particle .data labels and string/jumptable-adjacent objects gained scope:local metadata.
Renamed some particle BSS symbols in symbols.txt from anonymous hsd_804D0B50-style names to more meaningful names: psTexGroupArray, psNumCmdList, psCmdListArray, and ptclref.
Changed several BSS symbols near the particle block from scope:global to scope:local, including hsd_804CE3F8 through hsd_804D0F90 entries shown in the diff excerpt.
Touched dependent code in dballoc.c, eflib.c, and itseakchain.c to match the updated particle declarations/types.
Applied formatting cleanup after a rebase: ran git clang-format on particle.c, alphabetized includes, and applied header line-wrapping fixes to particle.h.
smart_moves: The author explicitly separated instruction-level correctness from objdiff percentage scoring and documented when a reported regression was caused only by .data offset relocation changes.

For fn_80392CD8, the author identified that new earlier source functions introduced string literals and jump tables before the function's MCC error strings, shifting the first string from .data offset 0x000 to 0x84 and changing mr r5,r31 into addi r5,r31,0x84.
The PR treated source-order .data layout as a translation-unit-level issue rather than trying to force a local per-function workaround that would conflict with further decompilation.
The author analyzed all remaining non-100% particle.c functions instruction-by-instruction and concluded the remaining gaps were relocation metadata artifacts, not fixable C-source mismatches.
Marking local particle data/BSS labels as scope:local in symbols.txt better reflects same-TU/private usage and helps future symbol hygiene.
Kept labels sphere_random and sphere_common because they were still referenced by goto statements, while noting an unused loop_end label was already absent.
Restored formatting/review cleanup after a force-push/rebase conflict instead of losing it permanently.
decomp_lessons: In large same-TU decompilation work, adding functions earlier in source order can move string literals and jump tables in .data, producing objdiff relocation/offset percentage regressions even when instructions are byte-identical.
For mwcc, .data items are placed in source order; partial TU decompilation can therefore temporarily worsen relocation matches for later functions until more of the TU is decompiled.
A byte-identical instruction stream may still score below 100% in objdiff due to relocation metadata differences, especially BSS symbol naming and string literal ordering.

Same-translation-unit BSS references may compile as section-relative .bss.0 relocations while the target or symbol map may show named symbols such as hsd_804CF810; recognize this as a tooling/mapping artifact before changing C code.
When all target and compiled instructions match, avoid churny C rewrites aimed only at relocation scoring unless there is evidence the source layout or symbol metadata can actually resolve it.
For big file-frontier PRs, document known objdiff artifacts in comments so reviewers can distinguish real regressions from expected partial-TU layout effects.
Header cleanup should follow implementation discoveries: remove stale duplicate declarations from broad headers and place particle-private declarations in particle.static.h where appropriate.
naming_conventions: Meaningful particle BSS names introduced in symbols.txt include psTexGroupArray, psNumCmdList, psCmdListArray, and ptclref.
Anonymous address-based names such as hsd_804D0B50, hsd_804D0C54, hsd_804D0D58, and hsd_804D0E5C were replaced where the PR had stronger particle-specific names.
Local data labels in the particle .data range retained lbl_8040xxxx or jumptable_8040xxxx names but were annotated with scope:local.
The PR preserved HSD-prefixed global names for exported/global particle objects such as HSD_PSDisp_8040C300, HSD_PSDisp_8040C360, and later BSS globals shown adjacent to the edited block.
TODO/comment formatting was normalized as part of final review fixes, though the exact TODO text is not present in the supplied excerpt.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Compared target versus compiled assembly instruction-by-instruction for all remaining non-100% particle.c functions.
Classified fn_80392CD8's 100% to 99.02% regression as a .data relocation/layout artifact because all 85 instructions matched byte-identically.
Recognized the specific codegen difference from shifted string offsets: a zero-offset base use can become addi r5, r31, imm when earlier literals move the referenced string away from offset zero.
Accepted that decompiling more earlier functions can temporarily perturb later functions' data relocations; the long-term fix is fuller TU decompilation, not reverting matched functions.
Tracked two recurring non-source mismatch patterns: .bss.0 section-relative same-TU relocations versus named BSS symbols, and different .data string literal ordering.
Used symbols.txt scope metadata updates to align symbol visibility with local TU data and reduce misleading global symbol treatment.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Confirmed the author's conclusion that the fn_80392CD8 regression would resolve naturally as more of the translation unit gets decompiled.
review_feedback.resolution: Accepted as expected partial-TU .data layout behavior.

review_feedback.author: malvarezcastillo
review_feedback.feedback: After a rebase dropped ribbanya's earlier formatting commit due to conflicts, the author re-applied formatting changes in a new commit: git clang-format on particle.c, alphabetized includes, and header line-wrapping fixes.
review_feedback.resolution: Formatting changes were restored; unused loop_end label was already absent, while sphere_random and sphere_common stayed because goto statements still referenced them.

review_feedback.author: malvarezcastillo
review_feedback.feedback: Final update said all remaining non-100% particle.c functions had byte-identical instructions and only relocation metadata differences remained.
review_feedback.resolution: Added explanatory comments, normalized TODO format, and updated stale match percentages.

follow_up_queries: Find later particle.c PRs that complete more of the translation unit and check whether the documented .data relocation regressions resolved.
Search for objdiff issue 333 to see whether relocation scoring for .bss.0 versus named BSS symbols or data-order artifacts changed after this PR.
Audit remaining hsd_804Dxxxx particle BSS symbols for opportunities to replace address-based names with semantic particle names.
Review particle.static.h and particle.h after this PR for private/public declaration boundaries that future baselib decompilation should follow.
Look for functions still below 100% in particle.c and verify whether their instructions remain byte-identical with only relocation metadata differences.
